title: fix(ci): [skip code-review] trigger not detected in commit messages
body:
## Problem

The `[skip code-review]` trigger in commit messages is not being detected by the CI workflow, even though `cr check-skip` correctly parses it when tested locally.

## Root Cause

In `.github/workflows/code-review.yml`, the `COMMIT_MSG` environment variable is empty when passed to `cr check-skip`, despite the "Get head commit message" step correctly capturing the message.

**Observed in PR #219:**
```
review  Get head commit message  [skip code-review] fix: address code review findings
...
review  Check for skip trigger   env:
review  Check for skip trigger     COMMIT_MSG:      # <-- EMPTY!
```

The workflow captures the message correctly but the variable isn't being passed through.

## Reproduction

1. Create a commit with `[skip code-review]` in the message
2. Push to a PR
3. Observe that the review still runs

## Expected Behavior

When `[skip code-review]` or `[skip-code-review]` appears in the commit message, PR title, or PR description, the review should be skipped.

## Workflow Code (current)

```yaml
- name: Get head commit message
  id: get-commit
  run: |
    echo "message<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    git log -1 --format=%B ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
    echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

- name: Check for skip trigger
  id: skip-check
  env:
    COMMIT_MSG: ${{ steps.get-commit.outputs.message }}  # <-- Not working
    PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
    PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
  run: |
    ./cr check-skip \
      --commit-message "$COMMIT_MSG" \
      ...
```

## Potential Fix

The GITHUB_OUTPUT heredoc syntax may have issues with multi-line commit messages. Consider:
1. Using a single-line capture: `git log -1 --format=%s` (subject only)
2. Base64 encoding the message
3. Using `actions/github-script` to pass the value

## Labels

- bug
- ci
